content: Hey, I'm Frankie, nice to chat with you. I just got back from a marathon in New York City and I'm still trying to process the amount of waste I saw on the streets.
Hi Frankie, congratulations on completing the marathon! That's quite an achievement. What did you think about the waste management in NYC?

To be honest, it was a bit disappointing. As a music producer and DJ, I've had the chance to travel to many cities, but NYC is one of my favorites. I've been volunteering at a local animal shelter on weekends and it's made me more aware of environmental issues.
That's great that you're giving back to your community. Animal shelters can be really rewarding to work with. Have you considered incorporating environmental themes into your music or DJ sets?

Actually, I've been thinking about that a lot lately. I've been brewing my own beer at home and trying to reduce my carbon footprint. I'd love to create music that inspires people to take action on environmental issues.
Home brewing is a great hobby! And using your music as a platform to raise awareness about environmental issues can be really powerful. What kind of music do you typically produce and DJ?
I'm mostly into electronic dance music, but I've been experimenting with some acoustic sets on my piano. It's been a fun challenge to blend the two styles.
That sounds like a great combination. I can imagine the piano adding a really unique touch to your electronic sets. Do you have any upcoming shows or events where you'll be performing?
Yeah, I have a gig coming up on August 15th at a club in Brooklyn. I'm planning to debut some new material and raise awareness about a local environmental initiative.

That sounds like a great opportunity to make a positive impact. I hope the event goes well and you're able to inspire your audience to take action. Will you be incorporating any environmental themes into your performance?
Definitely. I've been working on a new track that incorporates sounds from nature and highlights the importance of conservation. I'm excited to share it with the audience.

That sounds like a really innovative approach. I'm sure your audience will appreciate the creativity and message behind your music. Have you considered collaborating with other artists or organizations to amplify your impact?

I've been in touch with a few other musicians and environmental groups, and we're discussing ways to work together. It's been really inspiring to see how many people are passionate about making a difference.
It's great to see people coming together to address environmental issues. I think your music and message have the potential to inspire a lot of positive change. Keep up the good work, Frankie!
